Course Snapshot

Structured, hands-on learning path for Ethical Hacking Fast Track with detailed weekly outcomes and practical delivery.

12 Weeks
Beginner
Project-Based

Ethical Hacking Fast Track

Professional curriculum aligned to practical delivery, portfolio quality, and implementation confidence.

Duration: 12 Weeks
Level: Beginner
Study Time: 2 hours/week + labs
School: Hexadigitall Academy

Welcome to Ethical Hacking Fast Track! 🎓

This curriculum for Ethical Hacking Fast Track follows a Bloom-aligned progression from high-impact fundamentals to delivery-ready execution, with weekly evidence, labs, and portfolio outputs matched to beginner expectations.

Each week advances from comprehension and application toward evaluation and creation, ensuring progressive learning and capstone readiness.

Your success is our priority. By the end, you will produce portfolio-ready artifacts and confidently explain your technical decisions. You will graduate with a professionally curated portfolio that demonstrates scope, depth, and delivery quality. You will graduate with a professionally curated portfolio that demonstrates scope, depth, and delivery quality. You will graduate with a professionally curated portfolio that demonstrates scope, depth, and delivery quality. You will graduate with a professionally curated portfolio that demonstrates scope, depth, and delivery quality.

Prerequisites & What You Should Know

  • Hands-on experience with network protocols, operating system internals, and security control implementation
  • Practical knowledge of reading security logs, alert analysis, and threat detection workflows
  • Comfort with risk documentation, control decisions, and evidence-based compliance mapping
  • Familiarity with at least one SIEM platform, policy tool, or security scanner

Recommended Complementary Courses

Incident Response

Master triage, containment, and post-incident forensics workflows

Cloud Security

Extend identity, token, and workload protection into cloud environments

Governance & Compliance

Connect security controls to regulatory mappings and audit documentation

Essential Learning Resources

  • NIST Cybersecurity Framework, CIS Controls, and OWASP threat modeling guides
  • Incident simulation datasets, detection rule templates, and control efficacy checklists
  • Security architecture patterns repository and threat modeling workshop materials

Your Learning Roadmap

  • Early Weeks: Core controls, identity hardening, and baseline security posture
  • Middle Weeks: Detection engineering, incident handling, and service resilience
  • Late Weeks: Compliance evidence, executive reporting, and capstone defense

Detailed Weekly Curriculum

Week 12 hours + labs
Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Security Architecture Fundamentals (Sprint 1)
  • Identify the principles of Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Security Architecture Fundamentals (Sprint 1) and link them to course outcomes in time-boxed sprints with rapid feedback loops.
  • Explain Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Security Architecture Fundamentals (Sprint 1) in a guided scenario using realistic tools, constraints, and quality gates.
  • Apply trade-offs, risks, and decision points for Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Security Architecture Fundamentals (Sprint 1), then record rationale for stakeholder review.
  • Document a portfolio-ready control validation dossier for Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Security Architecture Fundamentals (Sprint 1) with measurable success criteria and next actions.

Lab Exercise

  • Apply security controls for Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Security Architecture Fundamentals (Sprint 1), including access boundaries and data protection baselines.
  • Run vulnerability or control validation for Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Security Architecture Fundamentals (Sprint 1) and triage findings by severity.
  • Implement remediation steps for Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Security Architecture Fundamentals (Sprint 1) and verify closure with re-test evidence.
Week 22 hours + labs
Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Identity and Access Governance (Sprint 1)
  • Identify the principles of Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Identity and Access Governance (Sprint 1) and link them to course outcomes in time-boxed sprints with rapid feedback loops.
  • Explain Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Identity and Access Governance (Sprint 1) in a guided scenario using realistic tools, constraints, and quality gates.
  • Apply trade-offs, risks, and decision points for Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Identity and Access Governance (Sprint 1), then record rationale for stakeholder review.
  • Document a portfolio-ready control validation dossier for Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Identity and Access Governance (Sprint 1) with measurable success criteria and next actions.

Lab Exercise

  • Design and apply role/permission structure for Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Identity and Access Governance (Sprint 1) with least-privilege enforcement.
  • Implement approval and audit workflow for privileged operations in Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Identity and Access Governance (Sprint 1).
  • Validate access paths for Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Identity and Access Governance (Sprint 1) using positive/negative test cases and break-glass controls.
Week 32 hours + labs
Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Network and Endpoint Protection (Sprint 1)
  • Identify the principles of Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Network and Endpoint Protection (Sprint 1) and link them to course outcomes in time-boxed sprints with rapid feedback loops.
  • Explain Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Network and Endpoint Protection (Sprint 1) in a guided scenario using realistic tools, constraints, and quality gates.
  • Apply trade-offs, risks, and decision points for Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Network and Endpoint Protection (Sprint 1), then record rationale for stakeholder review.
  • Document a portfolio-ready control validation dossier for Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Network and Endpoint Protection (Sprint 1) with measurable success criteria and next actions.

Lab Exercise

  • Build and validate topology for Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Network and Endpoint Protection (Sprint 1), including segmentation and routing intent.
  • Implement policy controls for Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Network and Endpoint Protection (Sprint 1) traffic paths and verify allowed/denied flows.
  • Run connectivity and resilience tests for Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Network and Endpoint Protection (Sprint 1) across failure and recovery scenarios.
Week 42 hours + labs
Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Application and Data Security (Sprint 1)
  • Identify the principles of Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Application and Data Security (Sprint 1) and link them to course outcomes in time-boxed sprints with rapid feedback loops.
  • Explain Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Application and Data Security (Sprint 1) in a guided scenario using realistic tools, constraints, and quality gates.
  • Apply trade-offs, risks, and decision points for Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Application and Data Security (Sprint 1), then record rationale for stakeholder review.
  • Document a portfolio-ready control validation dossier for Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Application and Data Security (Sprint 1) with measurable success criteria and next actions.

Lab Exercise

  • Apply security controls for Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Application and Data Security (Sprint 1), including access boundaries and data protection baselines.
  • Run vulnerability or control validation for Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Application and Data Security (Sprint 1) and triage findings by severity.
  • Implement remediation steps for Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Application and Data Security (Sprint 1) and verify closure with re-test evidence.
Week 52 hours + labs
Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Threat Detection and Monitoring (Sprint 1)
  • Apply the principles of Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Threat Detection and Monitoring (Sprint 1) and link them to course outcomes in time-boxed sprints with rapid feedback loops.
  • Analyze Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Threat Detection and Monitoring (Sprint 1) in a guided scenario using realistic tools, constraints, and quality gates.
  • Evaluate trade-offs, risks, and decision points for Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Threat Detection and Monitoring (Sprint 1), then record rationale for stakeholder review.
  • Document a portfolio-ready control validation dossier for Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Threat Detection and Monitoring (Sprint 1) with measurable success criteria and next actions.

Lab Exercise

  • Instrument Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Threat Detection and Monitoring (Sprint 1) with metrics, logs, and tracing hooks aligned to service objectives.
  • Create actionable alerts for Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Threat Detection and Monitoring (Sprint 1) and test escalation paths using simulated incidents.
  • Perform root-cause analysis for a Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Threat Detection and Monitoring (Sprint 1) failure scenario and document corrective actions.
Week 62 hours + labs
Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Incident Response and Recovery (Sprint 1)
  • Apply the principles of Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Incident Response and Recovery (Sprint 1) and link them to course outcomes in time-boxed sprints with rapid feedback loops.
  • Analyze Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Incident Response and Recovery (Sprint 1) in a guided scenario using realistic tools, constraints, and quality gates.
  • Evaluate trade-offs, risks, and decision points for Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Incident Response and Recovery (Sprint 1), then record rationale for stakeholder review.
  • Document a portfolio-ready control validation dossier for Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Incident Response and Recovery (Sprint 1) with measurable success criteria and next actions.

Lab Exercise

  • Instrument Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Incident Response and Recovery (Sprint 1) with metrics, logs, and tracing hooks aligned to service objectives.
  • Create actionable alerts for Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Incident Response and Recovery (Sprint 1) and test escalation paths using simulated incidents.
  • Perform root-cause analysis for a Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Incident Response and Recovery (Sprint 1) failure scenario and document corrective actions.
Week 72 hours + labs
Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Compliance and Audit Readiness (Sprint 1)
  • Apply the principles of Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Compliance and Audit Readiness (Sprint 1) and link them to course outcomes in time-boxed sprints with rapid feedback loops.
  • Analyze Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Compliance and Audit Readiness (Sprint 1) in a guided scenario using realistic tools, constraints, and quality gates.
  • Evaluate trade-offs, risks, and decision points for Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Compliance and Audit Readiness (Sprint 1), then record rationale for stakeholder review.
  • Document a portfolio-ready control validation dossier for Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Compliance and Audit Readiness (Sprint 1) with measurable success criteria and next actions.

Lab Exercise

  • Apply security controls for Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Compliance and Audit Readiness (Sprint 1), including access boundaries and data protection baselines.
  • Run vulnerability or control validation for Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Compliance and Audit Readiness (Sprint 1) and triage findings by severity.
  • Implement remediation steps for Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Compliance and Audit Readiness (Sprint 1) and verify closure with re-test evidence.
Week 82 hours + labs
Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Security Program Optimization (Sprint 1)
  • Apply the principles of Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Security Program Optimization (Sprint 1) and link them to course outcomes in time-boxed sprints with rapid feedback loops.
  • Analyze Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Security Program Optimization (Sprint 1) in a guided scenario using realistic tools, constraints, and quality gates.
  • Evaluate trade-offs, risks, and decision points for Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Security Program Optimization (Sprint 1), then record rationale for stakeholder review.
  • Document a portfolio-ready control validation dossier for Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Security Program Optimization (Sprint 1) with measurable success criteria and next actions.

Lab Exercise

  • Apply security controls for Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Security Program Optimization (Sprint 1), including access boundaries and data protection baselines.
  • Run vulnerability or control validation for Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Security Program Optimization (Sprint 1) and triage findings by severity.
  • Implement remediation steps for Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Security Program Optimization (Sprint 1) and verify closure with re-test evidence.
Week 92 hours + labs
Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Security Architecture Fundamentals (Sprint 2)
  • Analyze the principles of Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Security Architecture Fundamentals (Sprint 2) and link them to course outcomes in time-boxed sprints with rapid feedback loops.
  • Evaluate Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Security Architecture Fundamentals (Sprint 2) in a guided scenario using realistic tools, constraints, and quality gates.
  • Create trade-offs, risks, and decision points for Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Security Architecture Fundamentals (Sprint 2), then record rationale for stakeholder review.
  • Defend a portfolio-ready control validation dossier for Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Security Architecture Fundamentals (Sprint 2) with measurable success criteria and next actions.

Lab Exercise

  • Apply security controls for Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Security Architecture Fundamentals (Sprint 2), including access boundaries and data protection baselines.
  • Run vulnerability or control validation for Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Security Architecture Fundamentals (Sprint 2) and triage findings by severity.
  • Implement remediation steps for Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Security Architecture Fundamentals (Sprint 2) and verify closure with re-test evidence.

  • Evaluate Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Identity and Access Governance (Sprint 2) in a guided scenario using realistic tools, constraints, and quality gates.
  • Create trade-offs, risks, and decision points for Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Identity and Access Governance (Sprint 2), then record rationale for stakeholder review.
  • Defend a portfolio-ready control validation dossier for Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Identity and Access Governance (Sprint 2) with measurable success criteria and next actions.

Lab Exercise

  • Design and apply role/permission structure for Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Identity and Access Governance (Sprint 2) with least-privilege enforcement.
  • Implement approval and audit workflow for privileged operations in Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Identity and Access Governance (Sprint 2).
  • Validate access paths for Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Identity and Access Governance (Sprint 2) using positive/negative test cases and break-glass controls.
Week 112 hours + labs
Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Network and Endpoint Protection (Sprint 2)
  • Analyze the principles of Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Network and Endpoint Protection (Sprint 2) and link them to course outcomes in time-boxed sprints with rapid feedback loops.
  • Evaluate Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Network and Endpoint Protection (Sprint 2) in a guided scenario using realistic tools, constraints, and quality gates.

  • Defend a portfolio-ready control validation dossier for Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Network and Endpoint Protection (Sprint 2) with measurable success criteria and next actions.

  • Implement policy controls for Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Network and Endpoint Protection (Sprint 2) traffic paths and verify allowed/denied flows.
  • Run connectivity and resilience tests for Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Network and Endpoint Protection (Sprint 2) across failure and recovery scenarios.
Week 122 hours + labs

  • Analyze the principles of Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Application and Data Security (Sprint 2) and link them to course outcomes in time-boxed sprints with rapid feedback loops.
  • Evaluate Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Application and Data Security (Sprint 2) in a guided scenario using realistic tools, constraints, and quality gates.
  • Create trade-offs, risks, and decision points for Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Application and Data Security (Sprint 2), then record rationale for stakeholder review.
  • Defend a portfolio-ready control validation dossier for Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Application and Data Security (Sprint 2) with measurable success criteria and next actions.

Lab Exercise

  • Apply security controls for Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Application and Data Security (Sprint 2), including access boundaries and data protection baselines.
  • Run vulnerability or control validation for Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Application and Data Security (Sprint 2) and triage findings by severity.
  • Implement remediation steps for Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Application and Data Security (Sprint 2) and verify closure with re-test evidence.

Capstone Projects

Project 1: Ethical Hacking Fast Track Foundation Build

Deliver a concrete foundation implementation covering the first phase of the curriculum.

  • Implement and validate Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Security Architecture Fundamentals (Sprint 1).
  • Integrate Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Identity and Access Governance (Sprint 1) with reusable workflow standards.
  • Publish evidence for Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Network and Endpoint Protection (Sprint 1) with test and quality artifacts.

Project 2: Ethical Hacking Fast Track Integrated Systems Build

Combine mid-program competencies into a production-style integrated workflow.

  • Build an end-to-end flow around Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Threat Detection and Monitoring (Sprint 1) and Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Incident Response and Recovery (Sprint 1).
  • Add controls, observability, and rollback paths for reliability.
  • Document architecture decisions and trade-offs tied to Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Compliance and Audit Readiness (Sprint 1).

Project 3: Ethical Hacking Fast Track Capstone Delivery

Ship a portfolio-ready capstone with measurable outcomes and stakeholder-ready presentation.

  • Deliver a complete implementation centered on Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Security Architecture Fundamentals (Sprint 2).
  • Validate readiness for Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Identity and Access Governance (Sprint 2) using objective acceptance checks.
  • Present final defense and roadmap based on Ethical Hacking Fast Track: Network and Endpoint Protection (Sprint 2) outcomes.

Hexadigitall • Empowering Digital Futures
